accept_synthetic.justification:
Final verdict: NEEDS-EVIDENCE accepted with documented overrides.

== Synthetic-evidence overrides (5 declared [S], 16 auto-propagated [S*]) ==
The 5 declared [S] tasks (T029, T035, T037, T042, T046) are all gated on the
HighBarV3 proxy AI / game executable workstream which is owned separately and
not yet provisioned on dev / CI hosts. Every entry has a SYNTHETIC code-level
disclosure, a Synthetic_-prefixed test name, and a row in the Synthetic-
Evidence Inventory in tasks.md naming the real-evidence path. The broker-side
wire path is real production code in every case. The 16 [S*] tasks are auto-
propagated from those 5 via tasks.deps.yml + the implicit phase-boundary edges;
no [S*] surprises (verified in T049).

== Diff-scan false positives (7 blocking hits, all bogus) ==
1. .specify/memory/constitution.md:90  -- the constitution itself defining the
   "no NotImplementedException / failwith TODO" rule. Pattern matches the
   meta-text describing the rule.
2. .specify/memory/constitution.md:139 -- the constitution itself naming the
   [<Skip>] attribute as the disclosed-skip mechanism. Same meta-text issue.
3. scripts/prelude.fsx:17 -- comment text in the FSI prelude reading
   "return real results instead of throwing failwith "not implemented"".
   Documentation, not production code.
4. specs/001-tui-grpc-broker/readiness/task-graph.md:187 -- auto-generated by
   compute-task-graph.py; echoes T009 title verbatim.
5. specs/001-tui-grpc-broker/tasks.md:65 -- T009 task description recording
   that Phase-2 stubs were intentionally "failwith "not implemented"".
   Historical and accurate; the stubs are now real.
6. src/Broker.App/GameProcess.fs:82 -- regex collision: pattern xit\( (intended
   for JS test-skip xit(...)) matches the substring xit( inside WaitForExit(.
   Production code calling Process.WaitForExit(2000), not a skipped test.
7. tests/Broker.Core.Tests/CommandPipelineTests.fs:186 -- regex collision:
   pattern matching commented "should..." assertions matches "// shouldn't,
   but Invariant 3..." in a comment that begins with the word "shouldn't".

No genuine placeholder exceptions, no skipped tests, no commented-out
assertions. Verdict accepted.
